svg.text:
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ RETINANET + FOCAL LOSS │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
PROBLEM: One-stage detectors have massive class imbalance ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • Thousands of easy negative anchors (background) • Few positive anchors (objects) • Easy negatives dominate the loss
FOCAL LOSS SOLUTION: ────────────────────
Standard Cross Entropy: CE(p) = -log(p)
Focal Loss: FL(p) = -α(1-p)^γ * log(p)
where: • α balances positive/negative • γ down-weights easy examples • γ=2 works well in practice
▲ Loss │ 1.0 │ ╲ │ ╲ CE (γ=0) 0.5 │ ╲ │ ─────────────────── │ FL (γ=2) 0.0 │ ────────────── └────────────────────────▶ 0.0 0.5 1.0 p (confidence)
Effect: Hard examples (low p) get higher weight Easy examples (high p) get very low weight
